channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Is it safe to be outside in Hurricane Milton's eye? Here's what experts say
item.description: The eye of a hurricane is usually calm compared to the storm swirling around it — but is it safe to be outside as the eye passes over you?
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Deadspin loses bid to toss defamation suit over article accusing young Chiefs fan of racism
item.description: DOVER, Del. (AP) — A Delaware judge has refused to dismiss a defamation lawsuit against sports website Deadspin over an article accusing a 9-year-old NFL fan and his family of racism because of his game-day attire. The lawsuit was filed by California residents Raul Armenta Jr.
item.dc:creator: RANDALL CHASE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Las Vegas will blow a kiss goodbye — literally — to the Tropicana with a flashy casino implosion
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Sin City will quite literally blow a kiss goodbye to the Tropicana before first light Wednesday in an elaborate implosion that will reduce to rubble the last true mob building on the Las Vegas Strip. The Tropicana's hotel towers are expected to tumble in 22 seconds at 2:30 a.m.
item.dc:creator: RIO YAMAT, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Hungary’s Orbán interrupted by activist during EU presidency news conference
item.description: BUDAPEST, Hungary (AP) — An activist and municipal councilor for a Hungarian opposition party disrupted a news conference in the French city of Strasbourg on Tuesday, as Prime Minister Viktor Orbán was laying out his plans for Hungary's six-month presidency of the European Union.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Harris can't point to anything she would have done differently than Biden in 'The View' appearance
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Vice President Kamala Harris, in a Tuesday appearance on ABC's “The View," was unable to name a decision she would have made differently than President Joe Biden.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER and CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Courts keep weighing in on abortion. Next month's elections could mean even bigger changes
item.description: Two courts this week have allowed U.S. state abortion restrictions, and bigger changes could result from next month's elections.
item.dc:creator: Geoff Mulvihill, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Florida business owners bracing for Milton
item.description: The Doyles have 150 sandbags backed up inside the pub in an attempt to limit damage to the Punta Gorda establishment.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: States sue TikTok, alleging platform is addictive, harms kids' mental health
item.description: More than a dozen states and the District of Columbia sued TikTok on Tuesday, alleging the platform exploits and harms young users while "deceiving" the public about these dangers.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: New Border Highway Connector a ‘catalyst for growth’
item.description: SUNLAND PARK, New Mexico (Border Report) – Northwest El Paso resident Hector Dozal has seen traffic volumes soar in the past three years around Artcraft Road and Westside Drive.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, NewsNation

item.title: Trump campaign blasts Bob Woodward; denies secret Putin calls
item.description: Donald Trump's campaign is rejecting claims in Bob Woodward's new book 'War' about the former president holding secret calls with Vladimir Putin since leaving office.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Remains of Michigan woman, 68, who disappeared during vacation, found in stomach of shark
item.description: Remains believed to belong to Colleen Monfore, 68, were found inside the stomach of shark over a week after she vanished during a dive in the tropical waters of Indonesia.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: States sue TikTok, claiming its platform is addictive and harms the mental health of children
item.description: More than a dozen states and the District of Columbia have filed lawsuits against TikTok on Tuesday, alleging the popular short-form video app is harming youth mental health by designing its platform to be addictive to kids.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: This Montana Senate candidate said his opponent ate 'lobbyist steak.' But he lobbied—with steak
item.description: Montana Republican Tim Sheehy took a shot at incumbent U.S. Sen. Jon Tester during a recent debate for “eating lobbyist steak” while Sheehy was fighting in Afghanistan.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Brown, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Hurricane Milton may be October surprise in presidential battle
item.description: Strategists in both parties are looking back to Hurricane Sandy, which slammed into the mid-Atlantic states shortly before Election Day 2012.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What polling shows about Black voters' views of Harris and Trump
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Black registered voters have an overwhelmingly positive view of Vice President Kamala Harris, but they’re less sure that she would change the country for the better, according to a recent poll from the AP-NORC Center for Public Affairs Research.
item.dc:creator: MATT BROWN and LINLEY SANDERS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Harris reveals owning a Glock: 'Of course' I've fired it
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris said in a "60 Minutes" interview that aired Monday that she owns a Glock and has fired it at a shooting range in another example of how she and her campaign have made a point to highlight her gun ownership.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hurricane Milton to intensify and swell in size before unleashing on Florida and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Milton dips to Category 4 as Florida braces for impact
item.description: Florida's Gulf Coast braces for the impact of Hurricane Milton's winds and expected massive storm surge, which could bring destruction to areas already reeling from Helene's devastation 12 days ago.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Houston man fatally shoots autistic brother turning gun on himself in apparent murder-suicide: Sheriff
item.description: A Houston man shot and killed his autistic brother before turning the gun on himself in an apparent murder-suicide, the sheriff's office said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden sets a 10-year deadline for US cities to replace lead pipes and make drinking water safer
item.description: A decade after the Flint, Michigan, water crisis raised alarms about the dangers of lead in tap water, President Joe Biden is setting a 10-year deadline for U.S. cities to replace lead pipes and make drinking water safe for all Americans.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Daly And Michael Phillis, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Credit card rates hit historic highs as banks raise fees
item.description: The average credit card interest rate reached 21.5% in May, the highest level since the Fed began tracking the data in 1994, according to Bankrate.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Man falls 200 feet to his death at Utah's Zion National Park
item.description: A man fell to his death while canyoneering in Utah's Zion National Park. The 40-year-old man has not been identified but was with three fellow canyoneers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Diddy accuser’s lawyer: Combs used oil, knife
item.description: "My client was raped by Mr. Combs, his bodyguard, and friend who invited my client to his home to set up this whole situation.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Tampa Bay hasn’t been hit directly by a major hurricane since 1921. Milton may be the one
item.description: Hurricane Milton remains a ferocious storm that could land a rare direct hit on the populous Tampa Bay region with towering storm surges and turn debris from Helene’s recent devastation into projectiles.
item.dc:creator: Terry Spencer And Haven Daley, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: North Korea's Kim again threatens to use nuclear weapons against South Korea and US
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korean leader Kim Jong Un warned again that he could use nuclear weapons in potential conflicts with South Korea and the United States, as he accused them of provoking North Korea and raising animosities on the Korean Peninsula, state media reported Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Here's why experts are so worried about Hurricane Milton
item.description: Residents of the Sunshine State are no strangers to hurricanes, but experts are sounding the alarm over Milton.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Raccoon sparks chaos in stands during Broncos game against Raiders
item.description: Football fans packed Empower Field in Denver on Sunday to watch the Broncos take on the Las Vegas Raiders, but they weren’t the only ones in attendance.
item.dc:creator: Maddie Rhodes, Fox 5

item.title: The mayor of a state capital in Mexico is killed less than 1 week after he took office
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Relatives on Monday buried the mayor of a state capital in southern Mexico who was killed just one week after he took office. Photos of the crime scene shared on social media showed Mayor Alejandro Arcos’ severed head had been left on the roof of a pickup truck.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Federal prosecutors quietly dismiss Chasing Horse's long-dormant sex abuse case
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Federal prosecutors in Nevada have quietly dismissed a long-dormant sex abuse case against Nathan Chasing Horse, though the former “Dances with Wolves" actor still faces criminal charges elsewhere. The federal case was tossed Oct.
item.dc:creator: RIO YAMAT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Murdered Kansas moms: New charges filed in deaths of women on road trip
item.description: Three of the five people accused in the murder of two Kansas women earlier this year are charged with some new crimes and also had some charges dropped.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Video resurfaces showing FEMA prioritizing equity over helping greatest number of people in disaster relief
item.description: As FEMA fends off troubling allegations about relief funding, a webinar resurfaced of panelists discussing the need to reshape FEMA’s policies to emphasize "equity" over utility.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Harris pressed on changing positions, economic plan in '60 Minutes' interview
item.description: Vice President Harris was pressed on her changing policy positions and her expensive economic plan on “60 Minutes,” in what proved to be her toughest media interview since becoming the Democratic nominee.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: FEMA administrator continues pushback against false claims as Helene death toll hits 230
item.description: LAKE LURE, N.C. (AP) — The head of the U.S. disaster response agency continued to forcefully push back Monday against false claims and conspiracy theories about her agency’s response to Hurricane Helene as the death toll from the storm continued to climb.
item.dc:creator: JOHN RABY and GABRIELA AOUN ANGUEIRA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Many loathe public speakerphone calls, so why do people do it?
item.description: The fact that it's a phone conversation — not just in-person chit-chat — could be part of the reason people find loud speakerphone calls so grating, speculated Ozlem Ayduk.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, NewsNation

item.title: Salmon swim freely in the Klamath River for 1st time in a century after dams removed
item.description: HORNBROOK, Calif. (AP) — For the first time in more than a century, salmon are swimming freely along the Klamath River and its tributaries — a major watershed near the California-Oregon border — just days after the largest dam removal project in U.S. history was completed.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Are hurricanes intensifying more rapidly than ever before?
item.description: Rapid intensification is defined by the National Hurricane Center as an increase of at least 30 knots (about 35 mph) in maximum sustained winds within a 24-hour period, which can mean a jump of two categories on the Saffir-Simpson scale.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KRON4

item.title: Tossed balls from stands, apparently aimed at Profar, interrupt Padres' win in NLDS Game 2
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Tempers flared on the field and in the stands at Dodger Stadium, with rowdy fans tossing baseballs, apparently at San Diego left fielder Jurickson Profar, and then trash that caused a 12-minute wait between pitches during the seventh inning of the Padres' 10-2 win in NL Division S
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, Fox 5
